seo.title: Projects — Auth Fly | Open IdP, Hosted UI, Adaptive SDKs
seo.description: Five Auth Fly modules: a self-hosted SAML 2.0 + OIDC IdP, AuthKit hosted UI, AuthKit TS browser SDK, the Hanko adapter for the PoC, and the UI8Kit component library.
seo.keywords: Auth Fly modules, SSO IdP Go, SAML 2.0 provider, OIDC provider, AuthKit, AuthKit TS, OIDC Hanko, UI8Kit, hosted login UI, self-hosted authentication

hero.title: Open building blocks
hero.description: Each module is shippable on its own and composes with the rest. Provider-agnostic core, narrow adapters, no telemetry.

projects.name: AuthFly IdP
projects.summary: SAML 2.0 + OIDC Identity Provider sharing one signed session.
projects.tagline: One IdP session for SAML and OIDC
projects.description: Dual-protocol IdP written in Go: SAML AuthnRequest handling with ACS pinned to SP config, full OIDC authorization-code flow with JWKS, and a single signed idp_session cookie that bridges both protocols. Hanko provides credentials in the PoC; MVP swaps in Supabase or your own Postgres via the SSO SPI.

projects.name: AuthKit
projects.summary: Embeddable login, logout and error pages on Go + templ + UI8Kit.
projects.tagline: Hosted login UI, served from your origin
projects.description: Implements Renderer (RenderLogin / RenderLogout / RenderError) and ships static assets via StaticFS(). Branding, FlowConfig and FeatureFlags come from the host app; AuthKit itself never imports a credential backend, so the same UI works against Hanko today and other providers tomorrow.

projects.name: AuthKit TS
projects.summary: TypeScript core for browser auth flows, with thin per-provider adapters.
projects.tagline: One core, many adapters — start with Hanko
projects.description: The browser SDK core that AuthKit ships through SDKScript. A single TypeScript core handles flow orchestration; adapters bind it to a specific credential backend (Hanko first), so adding a new provider does not mean rewriting the UI or the IdP.

projects.name: OIDC Hanko
projects.summary: Hanko packaged as the PoC credential backend for Auth Fly.
projects.tagline: Hanko, ready for Auth Fly out of the box
projects.description: Hanko Docker image and config tuned for the Auth Fly IdP: JWT + JWKS for browser sessions, cookie-bound tokens, and CORS aligned with the IdP origin. Implements core.CredentialVerifier so the rest of the stack stays provider-agnostic.

projects.name: UI8Kit
projects.summary: Component library that fully replaces Hanko Elements across the hosted UI.
projects.description: UI8Kit is the component layer behind AuthKit: forms, fields, buttons, error states and the auth flow widgets. Bundled as a single ui8kit.js served from /static — no CDN dependency, no third-party JS in the login page.

intro.description: Open tooling shaped by protocol reality: signed assertions, JWKS, one IdP session — observable, documented, and boring where possible.
